title: Austromimusops marginata
taxonomy: Austromimusops marginata is a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the cashew family. This family comprises various species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Anacardiaceae family is further divided into 82 genera, with Austromimusops being one of them. Within the genus Austromimusops, A. marginata is the only recognized species. intro: Austromimusops marginata, also known as the Red Custard Apple, is a plant species in the family Anacardiaceae, native to tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Austromimusops marginata include its potential use in treating various inflammatory conditions, as well as its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and infections.
